## Hantavirus Cruise Ship Remains Stranded
The World Health Organization said Tuesday it suspects there was [human-to-human transmission](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2unDAUhJ_GdEb-wcYULiJFKM2mTYkO9mFxYjCyza54-4i7j3CbaUYzmm_-PKYHhBj252_Y0NYlTTu-S8RaMU8ewcfL5TMULGs4cjoral3qJJjQlCnK9OQhxGvqGm8XRIGyQcv73jDNB2Oa1UronGIIwBVfnDC9MV52egGFjmttmmDvNqaY5oxpYVrB5TArM_hBDrMDRTqGPtSU278p7HXFJV7phbl1aWuiXWs9CpE_iBiJGGF2N0H7TLdNxPgLIdaViLGc5UBX0dMV9gqvkM9C01nnjPCPwpwge_qBpTctXc8N9o_e8ZryReQYPJE_uRRa604NTbb-eIcYQ9E96dhzgxC_htVvvfmy4n8AAAD__3twiY8) of hantavirus on the _MV Hondius_ , a Dutch cruise ship stranded off Cape Verde, where so far three passengers have died, and four others are sick. [Footage from the ship](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOpCAUhZ9GOyZXVJSCYpvpZtstzQUu492gGEAn8_Yb9xX-8uQkJ-f7_ryWF3Lk_f0bNzI1pGWnT4lUK-XFE_r4dfnkQmXlI6ezklKlLhKkEjAKUItHjt9laL0JRJL6lkw3TTOoTs9zuxo1BW91hxMOIwJpGLwl0jpY3-NAoWVzr8EIqgNQcn7Irtd2nLXXvbYOx2YA8lxTfvxNvNeVQvymi_LDpa2NZq31KE3_q5HPRj7xuAHu6g65sovUyOeKe8WL81nETSIcHiQuyp7Edok17Z7PIkJKFd8knLK9tyPaDgIF6zU5Agu9omHq5tm22fjjwzFyUVMzwHtDjv_v1B85vIz8FwAA__8HDIbC) shows deserted decks and medical teams in protective gear while its nearly 150 passengers and crew waited another day off the coast of West Africa for evacuation. Cape Verde has refused to allow the ship to dock, but [Spain has agreed](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGOnTAQxvHTQDfI2NhAQZGGbtOmRAMer531w0_2sBa3j0iOkHZG-qT_79fH9oEhhvPzJ75oYZe2k2qJxEx5s4Q23ke-QqHiwzuni8mYwpsU0oDQIMxmMcR7G1q7OCJJqqWlH8dJmH6eptYv5PTgnFBmJ6PVcEgjUFA_o8ZROje1YXnWhBamF8LIqZO9mnc9zXZW836gbgZBNnDK3e8UTvbk4p2-KXdHerVx8czv0qgfjVwbudZauxt9Ss-3keuT85xTjraRK2YOR6RGrgqePAgnlKu86WCy4PFk_A75KpAu3jPhF-CeMFuwFx8e_lnAgwHVJyh4F_CUqUD1yFAJvs5UoSRwmKGftVDKjLrz_IptXuy7hhhDMWMziM8Xhvg3gv9L_nuRfwIAAP__I9GaLg) to welcome the ship at one of the Canary Islands, with the specific port still to be determined, where health officials would conduct a full disinfection of the ship. Hantavirus typically spreads through rodent droppings and was the same illness that killed actor Gene Hackman's wife, Betsy Arakawa, last year. Symptoms include fever, pneumonia, and respiratory distress.
## UnitedHealth Cuts Prior Approvals 30%
UnitedHealthcare announced Tuesday it will [eliminate prior authorization](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOpDAQRU9jMlBhwJjAwSZkvemGyNjV7dpxY2QXjZjTj6bnCJP94Os96f27LTdLkbbHX_tEw_e0bHiWiMyYF4_Wx8vlgwqWQHtOB6NShRcJUtUw1KAWbyleS195c0eU2FVo2nHUoNpJ6yoYO-qx733fghu0xrVTMLawYre23nl0FZlvGgygWgAldSPbbloHPfmpm1ZnB9EDeuKUm_-JNg54j1d6YW5celbRBOa9iO6PkLOQ83mezYruA3PZ7fVzEnJ-byHnYyNGH9BGDs5mrN3Bpd4zpVzbg0PK9GmZ0lbq9ao7EHKusvH7STFSUaPo4fG0FN9u_lWwl5FfAQAA__8yWYEr) for 30% of applicable medical services by the end of 2026, easing a process long criticized for delaying patient care. The change covers [select outpatient surgeries](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHSpCAQhJ9GMyxEAQ0ILtlsL73QQhmXuULxYFjLe_q_3Prf4I9mgq7u_vrPc3paDLi_ftsNDK1x2uHMAYggTQ6sC9eSCmbIHo8UC4FSmSbBhWJcMq4mZzFcU187swII6GowrdYDV-04DLU3qh_axVnQerX9vIpZcykldNp2el6HvkZzu3HJVcu5EkMj2m6c5TC6sRvnxcqq5-CQYmr-RtzJwxqu-IbULHGrg_FER666X5V4VOJxnmdTdiRwHmwg_0qxHLeyEo8bLcXPe0d-nw-HZMUvbCmU2ZEwJmYL-ZjwvyWMO0vwr2CCDXbKbL5Yx9kBaYGdGk9bqJNxx4khYFa66vlrsxg-9ehHm76N-AoAAP__rFyMKw), echocardiograms, certain outpatient therapies, and some chiropractic care, the nation's largest health insurer said. Doctors' offices spend roughly [13 hours a week](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpDAQRL8GMiNjwNiBg0vI5tILUYPbQ98ajEwzaPbrV8wvbFRBqZ6q6t9jfABF2p5_YUXHIY0bXkdEZsyjR_DxPeeTDjwW2nM6GbU-eFRSaSE7IfXogeJ7bEvvAqLCpkRX972RurbGlIvTMMk-GLStDkE3VqLFxvRqhrbrJmNKcjdNdlLXUmplKlU3duqM9bax0wxd0Ur0xClX_xNtvGCI7_TCXM1pLaNbmPejaP4UaijUcF1X9ZXT1t5uoYZ7zi3AlDaIhRrOjRj9ghB5mSGj4CQw0kobMIo9U8oCTl5Spu9PSoSURSNFCuLA_KIZb2KZnd8vipEO3RetfK5A8VOJf_Xjy6mfAAAA___DIYkE) on prior authorizations, the American Medical Association reports, and 93% of physicians said the delays harmed patient care last year. Major insurers, including [Humana and Kaiser Permanente,](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGupDAQRE8DmVFjwJjAwSZks-mGyGO3h14ZzNrNoNnTf_Gv8LOSqvRUVX8ey8NSpP31225oOKRlx6tEZMa8eLQ-flw-qWBZ6cjpZFSq8CJBKgGDALV4S_Gz9LU3AVFiV6Npx1GDaiet69UoGbzqYQpyDBK17Xs9BIAwBafvVE3mpsEAqgVQUjey7abnoCc_ddPT2aHqAT1xys3fRDuvGOInvTE3Lm11NCvzUaruVyXnSs7XdTXuWe4Vt1_J-ZaVnM-dGP2KNvLqbEbhTi6iA3FgdrizODKlLOzJa8r03zKlXWT8d1LGDXe-GXU2_rgoRipqrHp4bZbidw3-0XdvI78CAAD__4auhTw) made similar pledges last year to streamline prior authorization, but no further announcements from those insurers have been made.
## 1,500 Beagles Leave Wisconsin Facility
Roughly 1,500 beagles bred for medical research at Ridglan Farms in Wisconsin are [being released to rescues](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGypSAURFejGRagggYEk5i9SSe0eHDVO3MFC_A57v6Xfws_6qC7urrPn9f8skgY1t92B1OWOAe4MkEpkGYP1tPt0okZ8oZHimcBpXKZJZeK8Z5xNXuLdM9d7c0CIKGtwQitB67EOAz1ZhwfuIQOOIAWWutldHYUgoPotGydrNE8bbznSnCu5NBI0Y7vfhj92I5vZ_uq4-CxxNT8jRjKBgvd8QOpcXGvyWylHLlqf1VyquR0XVezxP_Pi8ev5HTmSk4J_Uo2sMWmPbM32JXg0RUDI7AfDCu7MLsYMj4ph4TlZgmyO4GtKZ5HZrkk_AfMgyWWgMBmqJPxx4VEmJWuOr7uFul7WPkRzY-RXwEAAP__RBqMMw), with hundreds already transferred to shelters in Florida, Wisconsin, New Jersey, and beyond. Big Dog Ranch Rescue and the Center for a Humane Economy paid an undisclosed sum for the dogs. [See the video of the rescued beagles](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2upSAUhZ8GOwxuBbWgmMbuTDulAdnonqCcAEfjPP3Eex_hNqv61k_Wn9f8MhToWH-bHXXxcT7wygFLwTQ7NC7cS_pQxrzRO8VPQaVymUGA4kJyoWZnKNxzVzntEQHbCnXT94NQzTgM1aYbOzjZA1qrrETXWjuoxdumW5xEK0VF-kkTUqhGCAVDDU07WjmMbmxHuxjJOoGOSkz130hH2dCHO56Y6iXuVdBbKe_M2l8MJgbTdV21-efNTuF-AAbTSQ4jg-mpYTAJ-QgwmDylXLiLa-YBzYk8kVuDObg3ac-8xM-y8TWZnPk3WmhH3kghHhMvyRzZY-IWVzoyg6lK2r0vCoGy6lkn1t1Q-JpZfvTtqeF_AAAA___HZI3u) touching grass for the first time. About 500 beagles remain at Ridglan, which is set to lose its breeding license on July 1, but [can still experiment on the dogs inside](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDEOnDAQRU9jOq-MAYMLF2noNm1KNNgDTGQwsoddcfuISDlBmmme5un__-s9vYEiHetP2NHxkqYDvyUiM-YpIIR4-3xRwbLRmdPFaEzhSSttpOqkMlMAivfUVsEtiBqbCl3d94MytR2GanOd9W0TDCwWDZpW-X5ura_73gbbhBYqco9NdcrUShk9vHTd2LkbHm5nD51oFQbilF-_Ex284RLv9MH88mmvotuYzyKaH0KPQo_HfabCDxJ6fMRCj6r7d64in35Cj3WnlJwR1ohFZiz-wiCXnHbJOV1zxCAzhTXCIRfIe5GwMGYJB-0QJXimDxUusnDK-_MKniLxLfRYZRfOL8VIxfSiVesOFP-G5f9a-OP0nwAAAP__CsSP3Q). Ridglan has bred beagles for research for more than 60 years and is surrendering its license to avoid animal cruelty charges.

Today's sponsor, , is helping you plan your ideal retirement with a free guide designed to align your goals, timeline, and investment strategy for lasting financial confidence. | Hantavirus Cruise Ship Remains Stranded The World Health Organization said Tuesday it suspects there was of hantavirus on the MV Hondius , a Dutch cruise ship stranded off Cape Verde, where so far three passengers have died, and four others are sick. shows deserted decks and medical teams in protective gear while its nearly 150 passengers and crew waited another day off the coast of West Africa for evacuation. Cape Verde has refused to allow the ship to dock, but to welcome the ship at one of the Canary Islands, with the specific port still to be determined, where health officials would conduct a full disinfection of the ship. Hantavirus typically spreads through rodent droppings and was the same illness that killed actor Gene Hackman's wife, Betsy Arakawa, last year. Symptoms include fever, pneumonia, and respiratory distress. UnitedHealth Cuts Prior Approvals 30% UnitedHealthcare announced Tuesday it will for 30% of applicable medical services by the end of 2026, easing a process long criticized for delaying patient care. The change covers , echocardiograms, certain outpatient therapies, and some chiropractic care, the nation's largest health insurer said. Doctors' offices spend roughly on prior authorizations, the American Medical Association reports, and 93% of physicians said the delays harmed patient care last year. Major insurers, including made similar pledges last year to streamline prior authorization, but no further announcements from those insurers have been made. 1,500 Beagles Leave Wisconsin Facility Roughly 1,500 beagles bred for medical research at Ridglan Farms in Wisconsin are , with hundreds already transferred to shelters in Florida, Wisconsin, New Jersey, and beyond. Big Dog Ranch Rescue and the Center for a Humane Economy paid an undisclosed sum for the dogs. touching grass for the first time. About 500 beagles remain at Ridglan, which is set to lose its breeding license on July 1, but . Ridglan has bred beagles for research for more than 60 years and is surrendering its license to avoid animal cruelty charges. Dear Patriotic American,

On October 25, 1994, [**Bill Clinton slapped a “gag order”**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e965685/VaHR0cHM6Ly9uZXcuaHNpbWVtYmVyc2hpcC5jb20vbTIvMzczMzE/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNjA1MDY/LbmNsQUJsRjQwNg/gafsWew/s0i71a9309a) on natural therapies.

After that day, the word “cure” quietly disappeared from medicine.

But thanks to the work of RFK Jr. and his MAHA revolution...

We can finally reveal a $5 medical breakthrough...

Vastly more powerful than any drug on earth.

👉 [**Every detail you need is right here.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e965685/VaHR0cHM6Ly9uZXcuaHNpbWVtYmVyc2hpcC5jb20vbTIvMzczMzE/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNjA1MDY/LbmNsQUJsRjQwNg/gafsWew/s0i71a9309a)

P.S. This “canceled” breakthrough shrank cancerous tumors in half in 2 weeks. Diabetic blood sugars fell 62 points. LDL cholesterol plummeted nearly 50 points. Joint paint disappeared, lung function improved, blood pressure dropped. Not hype... hard data. [**Every American over 60 should see this now.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e965685/VaHR0cHM6Ly9uZXcuaHNpbWVtYmVyc2hpcC5jb20vbTIvMzczMzE/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNjA1MDY/LbmNsQUJsRjQwNg/gafsWew/s0i71a9309a)

name: Andrew Sypher, Turning Point USA
subject: We’re trying to keep up
body:
They’re already claimed. And we don’t have extras.

| | | [](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyUkbuO3DAMRb9G7jzQw5aswkWAgbtNEQRIaXAoyiOMLSt67CJ_H3izP5CWPDwA7_31tr5B2EPcvsNBc65-zWX9oEw1_wlxq-eLKLW0Si51z8ee6wsQnZsdOhDY0SyMmbhWgzTdc_ZaIBqPVpEnrjnHaUArrJmkA1KuC_Ol4iPXgisuh5t9GHy4CaeRW-k8sIHDTrneMqX22ANCrFBe_sxINzyPbp-ftabC1DcmFyaXQtgy3SCSO-tFMLnUlmOIW5_OEGvfCjC5GDv6x2iot2RVP5CD3hKpHvzk_UiCNAFTSzlbRlrxdMTU_cfPRXChjWVSt3qsB7nQDqbudEDYv4b_Tpi6PyE7KK8V8PfXCuFIELbI1L2mVmAFrOE9lOMz0S7PLn2EfQ9FGzbw7ZJ-Pln_o4v3Wf4NAAD__8Hzlm4)
---
| | Friend,
---
I want to give you a picture of what's really happening right now -- not the polished version, but what it actually looks like on the ground.

Inside our office, tables are covered in supplies and open boxes. Coworkers are coming together, packing materials, sealing kits, and sending them out the door as fast as possible.
---
[](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyUkbuO3DAMRb9G7jzQw5aswkWAgbtNEQRIaXAoyiOMLSt67CJ_H3izP5CWPDwA7_31tr5B2EPcvsNBc65-zWX9oEw1_wlxq-eLKLW0Si51z8ee6wsQnZsdOhDY0SyMmbhWgzTdc_ZaIBqPVpEnrjnHaUArrJmkA1KuC_Ol4iPXgisuh5t9GHy4CaeRW-k8sIHDTrneMqX22ANCrFBe_sxINzyPbp-ftabC1DcmFyaXQtgy3SCSO-tFMLnUlmOIW5_OEGvfCjC5GDv6x2iot2RVP5CD3hKpHvzk_UiCNAFTSzlbRlrxdMTU_cfPRXChjWVSt3qsB7nQDqbudEDYv4b_Tpi6PyE7KK8V8PfXCuFIELbI1L2mVmAFrOE9lOMz0S7PLn2EfQ9FGzbw7ZJ-Pln_o4v3Wf4NAAD__8Hzlm4)
---
_Above: Activism kits bound for new chapters being packed with Constitutions, chapter handbooks, American flags, and more at TPUSA HQ in Arizona_
---
These boxes aren't sitting around waiting for someone to need them.

They're already claimed. And we don't have extras.

By the time one batch goes out, more requests come in.

Different schools, different students, same situation -- they want to start something, and they're asking us to help them do it.

That's where things stand right now.

We have more students stepping up than we can fully support at the moment.

And I don't say that lightly.

Because for years, the challenge wasn't getting students the tools -- it was getting them to speak up at all. Most kept their heads down.

The Left has spent years trying to convince this generation to sit down, stay quiet, and be ashamed of their country and everything that makes it great.

And for a long time, it worked. But that's all changing.

Now, we're seeing students stand up and push back.

**We saw it clearly at Wheeling High School in the Chicago suburbs -- Charlie's alma mater. **

For the first time, students there launched a Club America chapter. It took months to get approved, but a chapter is finally there.

And now, students are standing outside with signs about free markets and freedom -- not because it's easy, but because they've decided it matters more than staying quiet.
